_purpose: QA test tasks the developer (Claude) wants the operator to verify in the running app. Each task lists the feature, what to do, and what to expect. Operator marks Pass or Report Issue, which posts the result to the active chat channel for the developer to read on the next session.
_workflow: Operator opens Testing page → reads task → does the test in-app → clicks Mark Passed or Report Issue (with optional note) → result is sent as a chat message tagged [QA] which Claude reads next session.

tasks.feature: Native P2P push — group messages over the WebRTC mesh (increment 2)
tasks.what_to_test: Run TWO native instances with separate identities: launch the 2nd from a cmd window after `set HUMANITY_DATA_DIR=%TEMP%\hum2`. On A: create a P2P group, copy the invite ticket. On B: join with the ticket. Open the group on A (this issues/rotates the epoch key), then open it on B. Send a message from A.
tasks.expected: The message appears on B near-INSTANTLY — visibly faster than the ~2s poll. Send one back from B → instant on A. (Increment 2 uses host ICE candidates only, so both instances must be on the same machine / LAN.)

tasks.feature: Native P2P push — WebRTC DataChannel transport (increment 1)
tasks.what_to_test: Two native instances (separate identities via HUMANITY_DATA_DIR), both connected. On A, click B's name in the members list, then the 'P2P test → B' button (bottom of the members panel). Press F12 (Debug Console) on both.
tasks.expected: Both consoles show 'WebRTC: channel OPEN with …'; the receiver shows 'WebRTC: frame from …: native p2p test'. (Operator reported this working 2026-05-29 — re-confirm after any WebRTC change.)

tasks.feature: Web P2P group delivery (relay signaling fix)
tasks.what_to_test: Hard-refresh the web chat (Ctrl+Shift+R). Open the same P2P group in two browser tabs/identities. Send a message in one; open the browser console (F12) on the other.
tasks.expected: The message arrives noticeably faster than the ~4s poll, and the console logs 'P2P channel open with …'. (Before v0.314.0 the relay silently dropped the WebRTC signaling — `target` vs `to` — so the web mesh never actually connected and everything fell back to the poll.)

tasks.feature: Configurable chat timestamp format (native)
tasks.what_to_test: Native: Settings → Appearance → Timestamp format. Pick each option in turn (Time 17:42; +seconds; Date+time; Date+time+seconds; Full +milliseconds).
tasks.expected: Every chat message's timestamp pill updates INSTANTLY to the chosen format (already-shown messages too), all UTC. The choice persists across an app restart.

tasks.feature: System/deploy notices no longer leak into a P2P group / DM
tasks.what_to_test: Open a P2P group (native or web). Trigger or await a server/deploy notice (a deploy-bot announcement, a rate-limit message, or any [System] line).
tasks.expected: The notice does NOT appear inside the group/DM view (previously it showed there and then vanished on the next refresh — the 'deploy bot post in the group that disappeared' bug). On native it's filed under #general instead.

tasks.feature: Group history survives a re-key (multi-epoch decrypt)
tasks.what_to_test: In a P2P group, send a few messages. Then invite a NEW member and have them join (this triggers an epoch key rotation). As an EXISTING member, look back at the message history.
tasks.expected: Your earlier (pre-rotation) messages stay visible — they don't vanish after the join. A brand-new member sees only messages from after they joined, UNLESS the group was created with the Shared-history toggle.

tasks.feature: Chat reactions — pill display
tasks.what_to_test: Open #general. Find any message. Click the + pill to its right. Pick an emoji.
tasks.expected: A pill appears with the emoji + count 1, accent-tinted because you reacted. Click the same pill again — it should disappear (toggle off).

tasks.feature: Chat reactions — multiple users
tasks.what_to_test: From a second device or web client, react to the same message with the same emoji. On native, watch the count.
tasks.expected: Pill count increments to 2 without you doing anything. The pill is no longer accent-tinted unless you also reacted.

tasks.feature: Chat threads — reply
tasks.what_to_test: Click the ↩ button on someone else's message. Type a response. Press Enter.
tasks.expected: Banner above input shows 'Replying to <name>: <preview>' with X to cancel. After send, your message renders with a muted '↩ <name>: <preview>' line above its content.

tasks.feature: Chat search
tasks.what_to_test: Click the 🔍 button on the input bar. Type 'hello' (or any 2+ char query). Press Enter or click Search.
tasks.expected: Modal opens. Results render as cards with sender, channel, snippet. Click Jump on a result — it switches to that channel and closes the modal.

tasks.feature: Chat pins — pin a message
tasks.what_to_test: Click the 📌 button on any message. Then click the 📌 N button on the input bar.
tasks.expected: Pin count appears next to the input-bar 📌. Modal opens with the pinned message as a card showing sender, original timestamp, who pinned it, and content.

tasks.feature: Chat edit — own messages only
tasks.what_to_test: Send a message. Click the ✎ pencil button on YOUR message. Edit the text. Click Save.
tasks.expected: Inline editor replaces the text with a multiline TextEdit + Save/Cancel. Save updates the message immediately. The ✎ button should NOT appear on other people's messages.

tasks.feature: @ mentions autocomplete
tasks.what_to_test: Click in the input. Type '@' then start typing a username from the right sidebar.
tasks.expected: A popup appears above the input with up to 5 matching usernames. Click one — it replaces '@partial' with '@FullName ' (with trailing space).

tasks.feature: Right-click context menu on messages
tasks.what_to_test: Right-click on any message in the chat feed.
tasks.expected: Menu appears with: 📋 Copy text, ↩ Quote / reply, 📌 Pin message, ✎ Edit (only on your own messages), separator, 🚩 Report. Each action should work like the inline button equivalent.

tasks.feature: Server Settings — color-coded role tiers
tasks.what_to_test: Click the cog menu on the server row in chat. Confirm the page renders.
tasks.expected: Three sections: 🔴 USER (always shown — identity, role, invite link, disconnect), 🟢 MODERATOR (shown if you're mod/admin — kick/mute/ban target field), 🔵 ADMIN (shown if admin — lockdown, generate invite, channel CRUD, verify, promote, ban). Each section has a colored top-border accent.

tasks.feature: Onboarding page layout
tasks.what_to_test: Open the Onboarding page (top nav). Resize the window to different widths.
tasks.expected: Hero section centered. Headers (FOUR THINGS TO KNOW FIRST, THE PLATFORM, QUESTS) all share the same left edge. Cards stay within the centered column (1024px max). No off-screen 'Maps' card on wide displays.

tasks.feature: Aging system — life stage transitions
tasks.what_to_test: If your character has an Age component, watch its life_stage field over time (or fast-forward). Check Profile or any system that displays age.
tasks.expected: At thresholds (12, 18, 30, 50, 70 game-years) the life_stage transitions from child → teen → young_adult → adult → senior → elder. Log line fires on each transition.
tasks.note: Hard to test interactively without a long play session. Mainly here for completeness — log inspection counts.

tasks.feature: Combat — armor mitigates damage
tasks.what_to_test: If a creature with Armor takes damage, observe the Health drop.
tasks.expected: Armor.resistance per damage type reduces incoming damage proportionally (e.g. 0.4 kinetic resistance = 40% reduction on kinetic hits). Log line shows '(N% mitigated by armor)'.
tasks.note: Requires combat scenario to verify. Defer until combat encounter UI exists.

tasks.feature: Game state persistence across restarts
tasks.what_to_test: Move your player to a non-spawn position. Wait 30 seconds (auto-save tick). Restart the relay (`just sync` from a fresh terminal). Reconnect and check your position.
tasks.expected: Player position, game_time, and inventory are restored from the snapshot. Static ship layout reloads from RON.

tasks.feature: Perception API rate limiting
tasks.what_to_test: Run the AI sample client multiple times in rapid succession (or write a loop firing game_perceive every 50ms).
tasks.expected: After ~5 queries/sec, the relay returns game_error with type rate_limited. Bot should slow down and resume after 200ms.

tasks.feature: AI sample client end-to-end
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js` from the repo root.
tasks.expected: Connects, identifies, joins, perceives Crew Quarters with ~30+ entities, interacts with one, perceives again, disconnects cleanly. Should complete in under 10 seconds.

tasks.feature: Ambient maintenance_bot wanders Cargo Bay
tasks.what_to_test: Use the AI sample client (or game_perceive directly) while standing in or near the Cargo Bay. Take two perception snapshots ~2 seconds apart.
tasks.expected: A 'maintenance_bot' entity appears in the nearby_entities list. Its position changes between the two snapshots (Brownian wander within bounds).

tasks.feature: Ambient NPC crew per room — role-tagged characters
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js`. Modify it (or perceive multiple rooms) to peek at: bridge, medbay, engineering, cargo, hydroponics, crew_quarters.
tasks.expected: Each room has a unique role-named NPC: Helm Officer Vex (bridge), Dr. Kel (medbay), Chief Tan (engineering), CB-7 (cargo, replaces old maintenance_bot), Botanist Yara (hydroponics), Crewmate Nia (crew_quarters). All wander Brownian-style within their room bounds.

tasks.feature: Perception rate limit — per-action buckets
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js` to completion. Watch the perceive→interact→perceive flow.
tasks.expected: All three calls succeed without rate_limited error. Before v0.161 the second perceive was blocked by the shared 200ms bucket. Now each action (perceive / interact / query_inventory / query_entity) has its own 5/sec bucket.

tasks.feature: NPC dialog — crew talks back
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js`. Watch the interact response. (Or in-game: walk up to Helm Officer Vex, Dr. Kel, Chief Tan, CB-7, Botanist Yara, Crewmate Nia and interact.)
tasks.expected: The interact result includes 'speaker' (NPC name) and 'dialog_line' (a random one of 4 personality lines per NPC). Bot prints e.g.: NPC dialog — Helm Officer Vex: "Course laid in. We're holding orbit at 412 km." Other nearby clients see the dialog in the broadcast too.

tasks.feature: Browser page — curated bookmarks
tasks.what_to_test: Open the Browser page (top nav, blue group, between Testing and Bugs). Try the All / category filter chips. Click any card.
tasks.expected: Page renders with 5 categories (HumanityOS / Open knowledge / Real-world skills / Sovereign software / Spaceflight). Each card shows icon + title + description + host. Click opens the URL in your default OS browser. Hover tints the card and brightens the border.
tasks.note: Foundation for the in-app browser. When that ships, the same data file drives it.

tasks.feature: Two-tier nav preview — Reality / Sim / Tools / Settings
tasks.what_to_test: On any tool page, look at the right-most icon in the nav bar. Click `▤` to switch to the two-tier preview layout. Click each top category (Reality / Sim / Tools / Settings) and confirm the sub-tier shows the right pages. Click `≡` to switch back.
tasks.expected: Top tier shows 4 wide colored category tabs (red / purple / blue / gray). Sub tier renders the pages for the selected category. Reality has 14 pages including Profile/Chat/Wallet/Donate/Tasks/Market. Sim has Inventory/Crafting/Studio/Guilds/Trade. Tools has Calculator/Calendar/Notes/Files/Tools/Resources/Browser. Settings has Settings/Onboarding/ServerSettings/Testing.
tasks.note: PREVIEW. Reply via chat with: which 4 categories you actually want, sub-page reassignments, anything missing. Default still loads single-row nav each launch — flip via the [▤] toggle.

tasks.feature: Ambient NPC chatter — world talks to itself
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js` and let it idle for 60+ seconds after the demo completes (before the 20s safety-net timeout) — actually update the timeout in the script to 120s for this test. Watch the WebSocket for game_ambient_chatter events.
tasks.expected: Roughly every 45 seconds a random NPC broadcasts one of their dialog lines. Bot prints e.g.: Ambient chatter — Botanist Yara (hydroponics): "Lettuce harvest in three days. Tell the galley." Event includes entity_id, speaker, room_id, position, line.
tasks.note: Chatter only fires when at least one NPC has a non-empty `dialog` array. Skipped if relay is fresh and entities not yet populated.

tasks.feature: Nav layout preference persists across launches
tasks.what_to_test: Switch to the two-tier nav via [▤]. Pick a top category like Sim. Close the app fully (don't just reload). Relaunch.
tasks.expected: App reopens with the two-tier nav active and Sim still selected. Switch back via [≡], close, relaunch — opens on legacy single-row nav. Preference saved to config.json next to the exe.

tasks.feature: Starter quest — Explore Ship
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js`. Look for the Quest line in the game_welcome log. Then in-game: walk into each room aboard the Pioneer (bridge, medbay, engineering, cargo, hydroponics, crew quarters).
tasks.expected: Welcome shows: 'Quest: Find your bearings (1/N)' (1 because spawn room is pre-marked visited). Each new room entered triggers a private game_quest_progress event with visited_count + total. Visiting all rooms triggers public game_quest_completed broadcast.
tasks.note: Quest data lives on the player entity's components.current_quest. PERSIST_KEY bumped to v4 so old snapshots without quest data don't shadow new players.

tasks.feature: AI sample client tours rooms automatically
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js`. Watch the Teleporting → ... lines after the first dialog interaction.
tasks.expected: After the dialog test the bot reads game_welcome.rooms, lists unvisited rooms, and teleports through each one in order. game_quest_progress fires per visit, game_quest_completed fires at the end. Bot disconnects cleanly. Total runtime under 15 seconds.

tasks.feature: NPC greets player on first room entry
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js` and watch for NPC greeting lines during the room tour.
tasks.expected: Each new room entry fires a private game_npc_greeting event with the resident NPC's speaker + a random greeting line (e.g., 'Dr. Kel: "Welcome to medical. State your symptom, briefly."'). Greetings only fire on FIRST entry per room per player — re-entering doesn't re-trigger.
tasks.note: PERSIST_KEY bumped to v5 since NPC component schema added 'greetings' field.

tasks.feature: Quest chain — Meet the Crew unlocks after Explore Ship
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js`. After explore_ship completes, watch for game_quest_unlocked event then the new meeting_crew phase that talks to each NPC.
tasks.expected: Bot finishes the room tour, sees explore_ship complete, gets game_quest_unlocked for meet_the_crew, then revisits each room and talks to each NPC. Each talk fires game_quest_progress for meet_the_crew. After all 6 NPCs, meet_the_crew completes and bot disconnects.
tasks.note: PERSIST_KEY bumped to v6 since player components schema added a chained quest. Old snapshots get a fresh quest on next reload.

tasks.feature: Quest rewards — XP + reputation
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js`. Watch for game_quest_reward events when each quest completes.
tasks.expected: On explore_ship complete: '+100 XP, +5 rep (totals: 100/5)' with message 'You're getting your bearings...'. On meet_the_crew complete: '+200 XP, +10 rep (totals: 300/15)' with 'You know the crew now. They know you.' Player components.xp/reputation accumulate across the session.
tasks.note: PERSIST_KEY bumped to v7 since player gained xp/reputation/completed_quests fields.

tasks.feature: Third quest — Survey the Storage
tasks.what_to_test: Run `node scripts/ai-sample-client.js`. After meet_the_crew completes, watch for survey_storage chained quest. Bot tours each room and scans every storage entity (locker / cabinet / bin).
tasks.expected: Quest unlocked with goal = total storage entities (locker in quarters, medicine_cabinet in medbay, tool_cabinet + spare_parts_bin in engineering, harvest_bin in hydroponics, inventory_terminal in cargo = 6 storage). Each scan fires game_quest_progress. After all scanned, quest completes with +300 XP +15 rep, totals: 600 XP / 30 rep.
tasks.note: PERSIST_KEY bumped to v8 since equipment entities gained 'storage' boolean. Validates the quest framework chains an arbitrary number of quests.

tasks.feature: Nav RGB channeling separator + active button consistency
tasks.what_to_test: Switch to two-tier nav via [▤]. Watch the thin colored line under the top tier and under the sub tier — it should slowly cycle through the RGB spectrum. Click each top category — the active one's border should also be the same channeling RGB. Switch back to legacy via [≡] — should now ALSO have the channeling line under the single-row nav.
tasks.expected: Both nav layouts have a 3px-tall RGB separator line directly below them, slowly shifting through the hue spectrum. In two-tier: a separator under both the top tier AND the sub tier. Active top-category button (Reality/Sim/Tools/Settings) has an animated RGB border matching the active sub-page button. The channeling color stays in lockstep across all attention-grabbing UI elements.
tasks.note: Future: when player is being attacked the channeling color flips from cyclic RGB to a pulsing red. The state field exists (state.attack_pulse_active) but no combat system sets it yet. Hand-test with a debug toggle once added.

tasks.feature: Two-tier nav is the default layout
tasks.what_to_test: Delete (or rename) `config.json` next to the exe and relaunch — fresh install sims a new user. App should open on the two-tier nav (Reality / Sim / Tools / Settings + sub tier). If you've previously toggled to legacy and saved it, your choice is respected — flip via the [▤] button to revisit two-tier.
tasks.expected: Fresh install: two-tier nav active out of the gate. Existing config: previous nav_two_tier value honored. Toggle button still works in both directions.

tasks.feature: Nav colors editable from Settings page
tasks.what_to_test: Open Settings → scroll to color editors. Look for nav_reality, nav_sim, nav_tools, nav_settings, nav_legacy_red, nav_legacy_green, nav_legacy_blue. Tweak one — e.g. change nav_sim from purple to neon green. Save. Switch to two-tier nav and click the Sim category.
tasks.expected: Sim button + sub-tier color reflects your edit immediately. Same for the other nav category colors. Sizes (nav_separator_height, nav_active_border_width) are also editable as numeric tokens.
tasks.note: Backstop: cargo test --test theme_token_lint will fail any future PR that adds a new Color32::from_rgb(N, N, N) literal in src/gui/ or src/renderer/. 168 pre-existing literals in 22 legacy files are allowlisted with audit notes — those get migrated one release at a time.

tasks.feature: FOV slider no longer overlaps its value label
tasks.what_to_test: Open Settings → Graphics → FOV. Drag the slider. Watch the numeric value on the right.
tasks.expected: The slider track stops 56px before the right edge so the numeric value (e.g. '90') always paints in its own space, never on top of the slider. Same fix applies to every other labeled slider on the page (volumes, render distance, mouse sensitivity, all the Widgets sizing/spacing knobs).

tasks.feature: Nav color + size tokens visible in Settings
tasks.what_to_test: Settings → Appearance → scroll to 'Nav category colors' section. Settings → Widgets → scroll to 'Nav' card.
tasks.expected: Appearance shows 7 nav color rows (Reality, Sim, Tools, Settings, plus 3 legacy group colors). Widgets shows a Nav card with Separator Height, Active Border Width, Hover Border Width sliders. Editing any of them updates the nav immediately. Also: Background (modal overlay) is now editable — was missing.

tasks.feature: Settings editor coverage enforced by cargo test
tasks.what_to_test: (Developer-facing.) Run `cargo test --test theme_editor_coverage`. Then ADD a fake field `pub fake_color: C,` to theme.rs (don't wire it into the editor). Re-run.
tasks.expected: Initially passes (after this release every token is editable). After adding fake_color the test fails with: '❌ 1 color token(s) defined in src/gui/theme.rs are NOT editable... theme.fake_color'. This is the durable backstop against the v0.175 → v0.176 nav-missing-from-editor incident recurring.

tasks.feature: Animations settings section — pick your own RGB style + attack indicator
tasks.what_to_test: Settings → Animations. Try: (1) flip the master switch off — RGB cycling on the nav separator + active borders should freeze. Flip back on. (2) Change Nav Separator → Style to Solid; the bar should hold a single color. Try Pulse; it should breathe brightness. (3) Speed slider on each — tweak and watch the rate change. (4) Attack Indicator → click 'Test attack pulse for 3s'. Watch the nav. Then change style to Pulse Yellow / Flash White / Solid and re-test.
tasks.expected: Master switch freezes everything. Each style picker visibly changes the animation in real time. Attack test button triggers the configured indicator for 3s then auto-clears. Speeds at 0.0 = static, 1.0 = default, 3.0 = fast. All settings persist via Save Animations.
tasks.note: All animation behavior previously hardcoded in escape_menu.rs is now token-driven. Reduced-motion users can disable everything; action gamers can pick yellow-pulse over red. Five attack styles: None / Pulse Red / Pulse Yellow / Flash White / Solid Danger Color.

tasks.feature: Doc audit — CLAUDE.md / MEMORY.md / new docs/PAGES.md
tasks.what_to_test: (Developer-facing.) Open CLAUDE.md and look for the 'Cryptography (canonical, audited 2026-05-03)' table. Open docs/PAGES.md — should list 32 native + 38 web pages. Open MEMORY.md (`C:\Users\Shaos\.claude\projects\C--Humanity\memory\MEMORY.md`) — should reference single-crate `src/` layout, NOT the old `server/` `native/` `crates/` split.
tasks.expected: All three docs accurately reflect current state. Crypto table shows dual-stack (Ed25519 chat + Dilithium3 federation). Page registry has 32 native pages with file paths + audience. MEMORY.md anti-context-rot rules section explains how to prevent recurrence.

tasks.feature: Security fixes — H1 SQL pattern + H2 timestamp freshness + H3 federated chat sig
tasks.what_to_test: Operationally invisible — these are defensive fixes. Verify by: (1) cargo test --features relay --no-default-features (should show 201 tests pass, including 5 new freshness_tests). (2) On the live VPS, sending a federated_chat with no signature or stale timestamp should now be rejected with 'invalid or missing signature' / 'timestamp outside ±5 min window' in the relay log.
tasks.expected: H1: trading.rs no longer uses format!() for column names — match into static SQL strings instead. H2: federation_hello + federated_chat reject any timestamp outside ±5 min skew window. H3: federated_chat now requires a signature, verifies it against the source server's stored Ed25519 pubkey, rejects unsigned or invalid messages.
tasks.note: From 2026-05-03 security audit. H4 (DM crypto silent downgrade to plaintext) deferred — needs UI work for the user-warning modal. M1-M3 + L1-L2 from the audit also deferred.

tasks.feature: Dev top-tier nav category + page regrouping per audit
tasks.what_to_test: Switch to two-tier nav. You should now see FIVE top categories — Reality / Sim / Tools / Settings / Dev (amber). Click Dev: sub-tier shows Testing / Bugs / Agents / AI Usage / Files. Click Reality: should NOT show Bugs / AI Usage / Agents anymore (they moved). Tools: should NOT show Files anymore.
tasks.expected: Five categories visible, dev pages cleanly grouped under amber Dev category. Settings → Animations → Developer mode → Show Dev menu toggle hides/shows the Dev category. Color is editable in Settings → Appearance → Nav category colors → Nav: Dev (amber).
tasks.note: Dev visibility default-on during development period; flips to default-off at v1.0. Operator decision per CLAUDE.md.

tasks.feature: Chat reaction/reply/pin/edit buttons gated to hover
tasks.what_to_test: Open Chat. Scroll through messages. Confirm: messages WITHOUT reactions show no button row. Hover a message — buttons appear (reactions +, ↩ reply, 📌 pin, ✎ edit on your own). Move cursor away — buttons disappear. Right-click any message — context menu still works as fallback.
tasks.expected: Cleaner chat surface; the row of buttons only renders when (a) a message has at least one reaction (so the count stays visible) or (b) the cursor is over the message row. Discord/Slack pattern.

tasks.feature: Ambient NPC chatter no longer pollutes #general (or any chat channel)
tasks.what_to_test: Open Chat → #general. Wait 1-2 minutes. Previously you'd see Helm Officer Vex / CB-7 / etc. lines appear as system messages every ~45s. After this fix: nothing.
tasks.expected: Game-engine traffic (`__game__:`-prefixed system frames) is filtered out of chat in BOTH native (src/lib.rs) AND web (web/chat/app.js). Game UI consumers + the AI sample client still receive the events because they decode raw WS frames themselves; only the chat renderer skips them.

tasks.feature: CLAUDE.md crypto section: explicit migration roadmap
tasks.what_to_test: (Developer-facing.) Open CLAUDE.md → Cryptography section → Migration status. Should now have THREE clear migration steps (Account → PQ-primary, DM E2EE → Kyber768, Profile gossip → ML-DSA) with a callout that today's reality is Ed25519-primary while the goal is Dilithium3-primary.
tasks.expected: Future sessions reading CLAUDE.md cannot mistake the goal for the current state. The X-reply-style mistake (claiming Dilithium3 in marketing copy when chat is still Ed25519) cannot recur from doc misreading.

tasks.feature: Top-tier nav buttons land on category overview pages (Reality/Sim/Tools/Settings/Dev)
tasks.what_to_test: Switch to two-tier nav. Click each top category button (Reality / Sim / Tools / Settings / Dev). Each should now load its own LANDING page — header in the category color, summary line, then a grid of cards (one per sub-page) with a label and a one-line description. Click any card to jump straight to that page. Sub-tier buttons still work for direct navigation as before.
tasks.expected: Reality landing: 11 cards (Profile/Chat/Wallet/Donate/Tasks/Market/Civilization/Governance/Maps/Recovery/Identity). Sim: 5 cards. Tools: 6 cards. Settings: 14 cards (after v0.182 settings split). Dev: 5 cards. Click any card → navigates. Hover → card border becomes the category color.

tasks.feature: Settings split into 12 per-category sub-pages
tasks.what_to_test: Click Settings (top-tier). Settings landing now shows 14 cards (12 categories + Onboarding + Server Admin). Click Account → opens its own page (no sidebar TOC, no infinite scroll). Same for Appearance, Animations, Widgets, Notifications, Wallet, Audio, Graphics, Controls, Privacy, Data, Updates. Each page has a styled header (label + summary) followed by the same content the old single-page Settings had in that section.
tasks.expected: No more 1300-line scroll page. Each settings category is a real GuiPage variant with a real URL-shaped destination. Sub-tier nav lists all 14 (no parent/sub-button redundancy). Edits in Appearance/Animations/Widgets persist to theme.ron via Save buttons same as before. Legacy GuiPage::Settings still renders the all-in-one view for any code path that explicitly targets it (back-compat).
tasks.note: Existing draw_X_content fns in settings.rs were only made pub(crate) — no logic moved. Each new page is a 6-line wrapper in settings_pages.rs that calls into the shared content fn under a styled header frame. If a content fn needs new behavior, edit settings.rs once and every wrapper picks it up.

tasks.feature: Chat action buttons (+, ↩, 📌, ✎) float as a popup, no layout shift on hover
tasks.what_to_test: Open Chat. Hover any message. Action buttons should now appear as a small floating popup at the message's top-right with a shadow/border. Move cursor away — popup disappears. Crucially: HOVERING DIFFERENT MESSAGES SHOULD NOT MOVE THE TEXT. Previously hovering would push everything below down by 22px causing weird glitches. Reaction pills (when a message has reactions) STILL appear inline below the text — that's static info, not a hover state.
tasks.expected: Hover-trigger UI no longer changes layout. Cursor sliding from message text right into the popup buttons keeps the popup visible (slight overlap by design). Click + to open emoji picker; ↩ to set reply target; 📌 to pin; ✎ to edit your own messages. Right-click context menu still works as the always-available fallback.
tasks.note: egui::Area with order Foreground + RIGHT_TOP pivot anchored to row.right_top minus a small offset. Shadow + bg_card frame so it reads as a discrete popup. Reaction pills kept inline since they're static content (this message has 3 thumbs-up — known, doesn't change on hover).

tasks.feature: Avatar icons uniform size from theme.icon_size
tasks.what_to_test: Open Chat. Compare avatar circles across messages of different lengths (single-line vs multi-line). Settings → Widgets → Icon Size: tweak. Confirm every avatar updates to the new size uniformly.
tasks.expected: Every avatar circle is the same size, scaled from theme.icon_size. Previously the icon was sized as 38% of row_h causing single-line messages to get tiny ~6px circles while long messages got ~12px ones. Letter font is now full side_font, not capped to row_h - 4.

tasks.feature: Timestamp pill — replaces inline timestamp + reactions row + popup
tasks.what_to_test: Open Chat. Each message header now shows a small pill: [timestamp Þ existing-reactions]. Click an existing reaction badge to toggle yours. Hover the message row — an expanded panel opens BELOW the pill with: ↩ reply, 📌 pin, ✎ edit (own only), then a Þ separator, then 10 default reactions, then ∞ for the full emoji picker. Click ∞ → 40-emoji picker.
tasks.expected: No more separate inline pills row below messages and no more top-right popup. One unified pill that lives where the timestamp used to be. Hovering messages doesn't shift layout. Clicking reactions in the expanded panel adds them. Right-click context menu still works as fallback.
tasks.note: Phase 1 of the operator's pill design. Top-10 reactions are static defaults today; v0.185+ will add per-user frequency tracking + server-configurable defaults + custom emoji.

tasks.feature: Timestamp pill polish — opaque bg + sticky hover + right-of-pill popup + header row breathing room
tasks.what_to_test: Open Chat. (1) Pill is fully opaque now — no message text visible behind it. (2) Hover a pill — expanded panel opens to the RIGHT (not below). (3) Move cursor from pill INTO the panel buttons — popup stays open (sticky combined hover region). Click a top-10 emoji or a function button. (4) Header rows are now tall enough to comfortably fit the avatar circle — sender groups have visible breathing room between them. Continuation rows from the same sender stay tight together as before. (5) Click an existing reaction badge in the pill → toggles your reaction (popup may still be open from hover but doesn't interfere).
tasks.expected: Pill text never overlaps message text. Popup extends rightward from the pill, mouse can move into it without dismissing. Header rows are at least theme.icon_size + 6px tall (38px default) so avatars don't crowd the text below. Popup width estimated correctly so the sticky hover region encompasses both pill + popup.
tasks.note: Combined hover region = pill_rect.union(estimated_popup_rect). Cursor inside that union region keeps popup visible. Once cursor leaves both, popup disappears.

tasks.feature: Pill icons RGB-animated + broken glyphs replaced + onboarding cards no longer overflow off-screen
tasks.what_to_test: (A) Open Chat, hover a message. The function button glyphs (↩ reply, ⊠ pin, ✎ edit) and the Þ separator and the ∞ all-emoji button should now slowly cycle through RGB hues like the active-page nav border. Reaction emoji that previously rendered as squares (❤️ → ❤, 📌 → ⊠) now show clean. (B) Open Settings → Onboarding. Resize the window narrower then wider. The 'Where to go next' card row should fit within the centered 1024px column — no card cut off at the right edge anymore.
tasks.expected: Function icons in chat popup pulse RGB matching the global animation tokens (Settings → Animations → Active button border). Onboarding 'Four core concepts' and 'Where to go next' card rows wrap correctly when content would exceed the centered column width.
tasks.note: Root cause for onboarding overflow — ui.available_width() inside a horizontal-wrapped child can exceed the parent's set_max_width constraint. Fix: clamp avail.min(CONTENT_MAX_W) before computing per-card width. Root cause for icons — emoji like 📌 and ✎ aren't in egui's default font; replaced with ⊠ and kept ✎ (with caveat). Variation selector U+FE0F stripped from ❤️ and other emoji to avoid the trailing-square rendering bug.

tasks.feature: Nav redesign proposal — Reality / Sim / Tools / Settings
tasks.what_to_test: Read this proposal and react in chat: replace the current single-row nav (chat, wallet, donate, profile, identity, ...) with a TWO-TIER nav. Top tier: Reality (red), Sim (purple), Tools (blue), Settings (gray). Sub tier changes with selection. Reality sub: Profile, Chat, Wallet, Donate, Tasks, Market, Civilization, Governance, Maps, Recovery, Identity, Agents, AI Usage, Bugs. Sim sub: Character, Inventory, Crafting, Sim Market, Sim Maps, Quests, Studio, World. Tools sub: Calculator, Calendar, Notes, Files, Tools Catalog. Settings sub: Account, Appearance, Audio, Notifications, Data, Updates, Onboarding. Real/Sim toggle goes away — separate Wallet/Market/etc per category prevents accidental real-money mistakes.
tasks.expected: Reply in chat with: (1) which 4 categories you want, (2) any sub-page reassignments, (3) anything missing.
